title: Methods for detecting and quantifying cancer nucleic acid
abstract: Methods of detecting and optionally quantifying cancer nucleic acid in patient samples. The methods can include identifying one or more structural variants in cancer nucleic acid relative to non-cancer nucleic acid, designing primers that amplify at least one of the one or more structural variants in the cancer nucleic acid but lack a defined amplifiable target in the non-cancer nucleic acid, amplifying a test sample with the primers, and analyzing the amplification product for the presence of amplicons to thereby detect cancer nucleic acid in the test sample.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method of detecting and optionally quantifying cancer nucleic acid in a test sample from a subject, the method comprising: identifying one or more structural variants in the cancer nucleic acid from the subject relative to non-cancer nucleic acid; providing an assay primer set comprising structural-variant primers, wherein the structural-variant primers comprise one or more structural-variant primer pairs, wherein each structural-variant primer pairs is configured to amplify at least one of the one or more structural variants in the cancer nucleic acid but lack a defined amplifiable target in the non-cancer nucleic acid; amplifying the test sample with the assay primer set to yield test amplification product; and analyzing the test amplification product for presence of test-sample structural-variant amplicons from the structural-variant primers to thereby detect any cancer nucleic acid in the test sample. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the assay primer set comprises at least two structural- variant primer pairs. 3. The method of any prior claim, wherein the assay primer set comprises up to 80 structural- variant primer pairs. 4. The method of any prior claim, the assay primer set comprises at least two structural- variant primer pairs configured to amplify different structural variants. 5. The method of any prior claim, the assay primer set comprises at least 10 structural-variant primer pairs configured to amplify' different structural variants. 6. The method of any prior claim, wherein the identifying one or more structural variants comprises sequencing the cancer nucleic acid from a cancer sample from the subject and/or sequencing the non-cancer nucleic acid from a non-cancer sample from the subject. 7. The method of any prior claim, wherein the assay primer set further comprises control primers, wherein the control primers comprise one or more control primer pairs, wherein each control primer pair is configured to amplify a control target present in both the cancer nucleic acid and the non-cancer nucleic acid. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the assay primer set comprises at least two control primer pairs. 9. The method of any one of claims 7-8, wherein the assay primer set comprises up to 10 control primer pairs. 10. The method of any one of claims 7-9, the assay primer set comprises at least two control primer pairs configured to amplify different control sequences. 11. The method of any one of claims 7-10, the assay primer set comprises at least four control primer pairs configured to amplify different control sequences. 12. The method of any one of claims 7-11, further comprising: amplifying a cancer sample from the subject with the assay primer set to generate cancersample structural-variant amplicons from the structural-variant primers and cancer-sample control amplicons from the control primers; determining a cancer-sample structural-variant amount corresponding to each of one or more of the structural-variant primer pairs, wherein each cancer-sample structural-variant amount is an amount of the cancer-sample structural-variant amplicons amplified from the cancer sample with a corresponding one of the one or more structural-variant primer pairs; determining a cancer-sample normalization amount, wherein the cancer-sample normalization amount is an amount of the cancer-sample control amplicons amplified from the cancer sample with corresponding one or more, respectively, of the control primer pairs; and normalizing each cancer-sample structural-variant amount to the cancer-sample normalization amount to thereby obtain a normalization factor corresponding to each of the one or more structural-variant primer pairs. 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising: determining a test-sample structural-variant amount corresponding to each of the one or more of the structural-variant primer pairs, wherein each test-sample structural-variant amount is 

an amount of test-sample structural-variant amplicons amplified from the test sample with a corresponding one of the one or more structural-variant primer pairs; and normalizing each test-sample structural -variant amount to its corresponding normalization factor to thereby obtain a normalization-factor-adjusted test-sample structural- variant amount corresponding to each test-sample structural-variant amount. 14. The method of claim 13, further comprising: determining a cancer-sample control amount corresponding to each of one or more of the control primer pairs, wherein each cancer-sample control amount is an amount of the cancersample control amplicons amplified from the cancer sample with a corresponding one of the one or more control primer pairs; normalizing each cancer-sample control amount to the cancer-sample normalization amount to thereby obtain a normalization factor corresponding to each of the one or more control primer pairs; determining a test-sample control amount corresponding to each of the one or more of the control primer pairs, wherein each test-sample control amount is an amount of test-sample control amplicons amplified from the test sample with a corresponding one of the one or more control primer pairs; normalizing each test-sample control amount to its corresponding normalization factor to thereby obtain a normalization-factor- adjusted test-sample control amount corresponding to each test-sample control amount; and normalizing one or more of the normalization -factor-adjusted test-sample structural- variant amounts to the normalization-factor-adjusted test-sample control amount to thereby obtain a cancer-nucleic-acid fraction in the test sample. 15. The method of any one of claims 12-14, wherein the amplifying the cancer sample with the assay primer set comprises amplifying the cancer sample with the entire assay primer set in a multiplex reaction. 16. The method of any one of claims 12-15, wherein the amplifying the cancer sample with the assay primer set comprises amplifying the cancer sample to exponential phase with the assay primer set. 17. The method of any prior claim, wherein the amplifying the test sample with the assay primer set comprises amplifying the test sample with the entire assay primer set in a multiplex reaction. 18. The method of any prior claim, wherein the amplifying the test sample with the assay primer set comprises amplifying the test sample to exponential phase wi th the assay primer set. 19. The method of any prior claim, wherein the test sample comprises nucleic acid obtained from the subject after being treated for cancer. 20. The method of any prior claim, wherein the test sample comprises cell-free DNA from the subj ect. 21. The method of any prior claim, wherein the analyzing the test amplification product comprises sequencing the test amplification product. 22. The method of any prior claim, wherein the test sample comprises the cancer nucleic acid in a fraction relative to the non-cancer nucleic acid less than 0.01% and the cancer nucleic acid is detected. 23. The method of any prior claim, wherein the test sample comprises total DNA in an amount less than 50 ng.
